item.title: Josh Hazlewood helps Royal Challengers Bengaluru to IPL win over Lucknow
item.description: Josh Hazlewood has produced another masterful display in the IPL to rocket Royal Challengers Bengaluru back to the top of the league.

item.title: From a new Australian musical to buzzy horror, here's what's in cinemas in April
item.description: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

item.title: US jury finds Ticketmaster and Live Nation have anticompetitive monopoly
item.description: A New York jury finds concert giant Live Nation and its Ticketmaster subsidiary have a harmful monopoly over big concert venues.

item.title: Fuel-saving campaign prompts open letter to prime minister from regional Queensland council
item.description: A Queensland council sends an open letter to the Albanese government calling for a national fuel tracker to protect rural economies, while criticising the federal fuel-saving campaign.

item.title: Investigation launched into Papua New Guinea Defence Force recruitment processes following soldier protest
item.description: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

item.title: Some country towns could lose more than half of their shade due to aging trees
item.description: Some country towns could lose up to 80 per cent of their shade due to aging trees, but replacing them is not simple for councils.

item.title: Australia's rarest reptile, the kungaka skink, found in far west NSW national park
item.description: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

item.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in seat of Narungga won by One Nation
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

item.title: NRL six-again rule should be scrapped, according to coach Ricky Stuart
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says the NRL has lost the fabric of its game, leading to anger over the league's controversial six-again rule change.

item.title: Farmers 'running on empty' welcome arrival of diesel to the Port of Newcastle
item.description: A massive crude oil tanker laden with diesel has docked in the Port of Newcastle to replenish regional New South Wales supplies, as the Middle East unrest continues.

item.title: Administrators tell Liberty Bell Bay workers to take leave without pay or be sacked
item.description: About 175 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter, Liberty Bell Bay, have been told they will be laid off at the end of next week unless they take leave without pay.

item.title: How to tell if your dog is in pain
item.description: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

item.title: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz open, thousands of Lebanese return home — as it happened
item.description: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, as thousands of displaced people in Lebanon are returning to their homes following a 10-day ceasefire deal with Israel.

item.title: Donald Trump announces 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon
item.description: The US president says he "had excellent conversations" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: Consumers are cutting back on spending amid higher risk of global recession
item.description: As fuel costs surge and supply fears grow, businesses and households say the economic shock from the war in the Middle East is already hitting home.

item.title: AFL-backed helmet company GameGear erases concussion protection claims
item.description: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

item.title: Myanmar's coup leader seeks legitimacy as family wealth surfaces in Thailand
item.description: As the Myanmar military carried out air strikes during a brutal civil war, the junta chief's family quietly purchased luxury real estate in Thailand.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins without Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire, but Israel's military will remain on Lebanese territory, meaning Hezbollah's conditions have not been met.

item.title: WA state guardianship and administration to be investigated in parliamentary inquiry
item.description: The state agencies which control the lives of some of Western Australia's most vulnerable people are set to be scrutinised in a parliamentary inquiry, with public submissions now open.

item.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism
item.description: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars this year.

item.title: Kulbanyi bush tea from NT station hits the shelves in Asia
item.description: The product is being sent from a remote property in the Northern Territory all the way to Japan, Korea and China.

item.title: Endangered southern brown bandicoots bounce back at Brown Hill Creek
item.description: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

item.title: Southern brown bandicoot filmed at Brownhill Creek Recreation Park
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Refinery blaze will not bump Australia to stage three of national fuel response
item.description: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

item.title: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger killed in Salzburg after car hit by train
item.description: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger, who played for Arsenal and a string of Italian clubs, died on Thursday at the age of 48 after the car he was driving was hit by a train at a crossing near Salzburg.

item.title: Before the timer reaches zero, 100 bombs have dropped
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces labelled it Operation Eternal Darkness. Inside Lebanon, it's now called Black Wednesday. ABC NEWS Verify has geolocated the explosions across 10 minutes of chaos.

item.title: By-election called for seat of Stafford in Brisbane's north after Jimmy Sullivan's death
item.description: Former Member for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan was found dead at his home earlier this month, after police were called to reports of a non-suspicious death.

item.title: National Indigenous Cricket Championships offers pathways for young players
item.description: The National Indigenous Cricket Championships, held in Mackay for the second year, has become one of the key development pathways for young talent.

item.title: Taxi drivers say fuel crisis another blow to industry
item.description: Country taxi drivers say the fuel crisis could prove to be the death knell for the industry in regional areas.

item.title: Keir Starmer looks to Europe as war in Iran further fractures US relationship
item.description: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

item.title: Makers of As Deep as the Grave defend use of AI-generated Val Kilmer in movie
item.description: A trailer for a film with an AI-generated character in the likeness of the late Val Kilmer has been slammed by social media users, but the filmmakers are defending their work.

item.title: Victorian political donations website goes dark after High Court struck out election law
item.description: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

item.title: Government advisory group recommends delaying NDIS 'robo-planning' changes
item.description: The federal government has been urged by its own NDIS advisory group to delay the rollout of a controversial new way of determining support plans for participants.

item.title: Mental health nurse Adam McGovern jailed for sexually abusing 14-year-old patient
item.description: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

item.title: Viva Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem for us, Tasmanian government says
item.description: Tasmania has 100 days of petrol and 58 days of diesel supply available, the government says — and that the Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem.

item.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages
item.description: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

item.title: Albanese joins meeting of leaders to talk about reopening the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Anthony Albanese has joined a virtual meeting of world leaders to discuss how the Strait of Hormuz can be secured after the conflict in the Middle East ends.

item.title: Parents of Aboriginal teen share anguish during sentence hearing of convicted police officer
item.description: The parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after colliding with a police car, share their emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing begins.

item.title: Aung San Suu Kyi to be freed earlier as Myanmar releases ex-president Win Myint from jail
item.description: Myanmar's ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi has her prison sentence cut as thousands of prisoners are freed. Meanwhile, the UN reveals 2025 marked the deadliest year on record for Rohingya refugees fleeing Myanmar.

item.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open as Lebanon ceasefire begins
item.description: US President Donald Trump immediately welcomed the news from Tehran but went on to warn that the US blockade of Iran's ports would continue.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Iran war and energy shock make delivering the federal budget unusually hard
item.description: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

item.title: Middle East conflict creates volatile airfare market as prices fluctuate
item.description: Australians are finding domestic flights for as little as $65 on some routes, while others are paying thousands to travel interstate as airlines quietly reshape the market in response to conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains tax changes
item.description: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closes as US blockade continues: Iranian official — as it happened
item.description: Iran has labelled the ongoing US blockade of the strait as a violation of the ceasefire agreement, while Donald Trump says "very good conversations are going on" between the two countries.

item.title: Disarming Hezbollah in Lebanon is unlikely to be as easy as Donald Trump suggests
item.description: Donald Trump sold a ceasefire in Lebanon as a step towards peace. But that promise hinges on a condition that Lebanon may not be able to deliver.

item.title: Coorong fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals
item.description: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

item.title: 'Derek' the dingo spotted roaming in Eastside
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lone dingo 'Derek' a concern for some as he roams suburbs in Alice Springs
item.description: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

item.title: How one walk could spark a national conversation on Australia's past
item.description: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

item.title: Man hospitalised after machete attack at popular Melbourne shopping strip
item.description: Police say a group of men approached and allegedly stabbed an 18-year-old man at the Eaton Mall in Oakleigh.

item.title: Marie-Louise Eta takes charge of Union Berlin as first female head coach in Europe's big five leagues
item.description: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

item.title: Social croquet connects communities as clubs record jump in numbers
item.description: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

item.title: How Maltese migrants united to shape Mackay's sugarcane legacy
item.description: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

item.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval
item.description: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran could end without a long-term deal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Traffic map appears to show ships passing through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Convoy of tankers seen leaving Strait of Hormuz on tracking map
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wallaroos beaten by USA in Pacific Four Series after Kansas City storm delay
item.description: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

item.title: Ready for Hard Quiz success? It's time to temper those expectations
item.description: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

item.title: Today in history, April 19: Good News Week premiered 30 years ago
item.description: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

item.title: How the perfect mother myth was unravelled by a family mystery in Italy
item.description: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

item.title: Why Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration is raising eyebrows
item.description: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

item.title: From celebration to calls of weakness, how the Trump-Pope Leo relationship soured
item.description: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the past year.

item.title: NDIS unregistered provider crackdown coming, as Butler searches for more than $6b in savings
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

item.title: The Australians keeping a century of rainfall records as stations disappear
item.description: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

item.title: The Solomons Six were recruited to a 'catch and kill' squad in WWII — their stories were lost to history until now
item.description: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

item.title: 'Old bushie' Steve Hawe has a dream to make his board game an Aussie classic
item.description: Steve Hawe says he never set out to make a board game — but when inspiration struck, he couldn't ignore it.

item.title: Pale octopus species mysteriously disappears from SA's Spencer Gulf
item.description: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

item.title: Women's shelters turn curated second-hand fashion into fundraising for frontlines services
item.description: Women's shelters are having to get creative to acquire necessary funding such as turning fashion into sustaining frontline support.

item.title: Record earthquake near Cadia gold mine sparks calls for more monitors
item.description: The magnitude 4.5-earthquake near Orange was logged as the region's largest on record, but only thanks to public monitoring data far away from the epicentre.

item.title: Young people taking initiative to quit at Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program
item.description: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

item.title: Head coach of the Tasmania Devils Women's VFL side's tarot reading pastime
item.description: Georgia Walker describes herself as a psychic medium, which she combines with her skills as a counsellor and teacher, as head coach of the Tasmania Devils' women's VFL team.

item.title: Fantasy becomes reality for next-gen speculative fiction authors riding self-publishing boom
item.description: Is your dream to become the next JK Rowling or Stephanie Meyer? A new wave of writers is turning to self-publishing to turn fantasy into reality.

item.title: Crime scene declared after body found in burning car in Scenic Rim region
item.description: Emergency services were called to the scene just before 6am on Sunday morning where a body was found in a burning car.

item.title: Thousands of dollars worth of belongings stolen from cars shipped across Australia, but no-one held responsible
item.description: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

item.title: Couple makes handmade skis out of Australian-grown timber in NSW Snowy Mountains
item.description: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

item.title: Iran's reclosure of the Strait of Hormuz 'disappointing', Marles says
item.description: The deputy prime minister says "every diplomatic angle" should be used to secure a permanent ceasefire in the Middle East.

item.title: Love story: I fell in love with my daughter's father two years after she was born
item.description: After a casual fling led to a surprise pregnancy in my mid-20s, I was parenting alone. A phone call two years later changed everything.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closed
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Demons cling on for epic win over Lions as Freo makes light work of Eagles
item.description: Melbourne digs deep to complete a thrilling two-point victory after a sensational tussle with Brisbane, while Fremantle claims Western Derby bragging rights with a 56-point humbling of West Coast.

item.title: Carlton aware Elijah Hollands was 'struggling in the game' as AFL demands answers after mental health episode
item.description: Carlton chief executive Graham Wright has "at this stage" ruled out alcohol and illicit drugs being a factor in Elijah Hollands' MCG mental health episode.

item.title: Newcastle Jets claim maiden A-League Men's premiership after Central Coast shocks Auckland
item.description: Newcastle has claimed a maiden A-League Men's Premiers Plate after local rivals Central Coast stunned second-placed Auckland.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 20: Can you tame our tricky questions?
item.description: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

item.title: With Iran and the Strait of Hormuz still in flux, China's bet on renewable energy is paying off
item.description: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy, China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

item.title: Australia's 'lucky country' mantle has slipped — and we're not happy about it
item.description: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Peter and Rosie use power of song and an appearance on The Piano to save endangered language
item.description: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

item.title: Can you ask the council to cut down a tree?
item.description: Expert advice on how to handle council trees causing problems on private property.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closure has Russia poised to capitalise on global hunger crisis
item.description: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

item.title: Is free public transport enough to convert motorists? Research says there are more important factors
item.description: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

item.title: Louisiana domestic violence shooting leaves eight children dead in Shreveport
item.description: The victims, who were shot at two homes in the US state of Louisiana, ranged in age from one to about 14 years old.

item.title: US delegation led by Vance to go to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, says Trump
item.description: A US delegation will head to Pakistan for potential peace talks, Donald Trump says, adding that he assumes "nobody's playing games".

item.title: How ex-NRL star Zac Lomax fared in first Super Rugby Pacific start for Western Force
item.description: Even if Zac Lomax's starting debut for the Western Force had been disastrous, it would still deserve to be considered a success.

item.title: Australia women lose Hong Kong rugby sevens final to New Zealand, men finish sixth
item.description: The Black Ferns Sevens beat Australia's women's rugby sevens team to win the Hong Kong title, marking the ninth time in the past 10 finals that the Kiwis have come out on top.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to shortages caused by war in Iran
item.description: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

item.title: Plans emerge for country's biggest AI data centre in Western Australia
item.description: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

item.title: Cameron Green victim of miracle stumping in IPL, Cooper Connolly powers Punjab Kings to another win
item.description: The losing run for Cameron Green's Kolkata in the IPL finally ends, but the Australian is undone by a spectacular stumping from Dhruv Jurel, while compatriot Cooper Connolly explodes for the Punjab Kings.

item.title: Shane Flanagan out as St George Illawarra Dragons coach after winless start to NRL season
item.description: Shane Flanagan and the St George Illawarra Dragons part ways after a winless start to 2026 extends an 11-game losing streak dating back to last August.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to war in Iran
item.description: Listen to ABC News interviews and commentary and analysis from radio programs like AM, PM and The World Today.

item.title: Kakadu Aboriginal corporation to take over as operator of Jabiru Airport
item.description: Jabiru Airport had been slated for demolition in October this year, but after months of uncertainty, a new operator has emerged to take over.

item.title: US capture of Iranian cargo ship puts ceasefire at risk ahead of peace talks
item.description: Donald Trump says the Iranian-flagged ship ignored warnings from the US Navy, but Iran says the action is piracy and breaches the ceasefire terms.

item.title: US marines rappelling onto Iranian-flagged vessel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA prospector discovers rare cosmic debris in 'the middle of nowhere'
item.description: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

item.title: Soaring airfares and reduced flights force some Australians to reconsider holiday and work plans
item.description: The war in the Middle East has caused chaos across some of the world's busiest aviation regions, with many Australians now reconsidering their travel plans — or abandoning them altogether.

item.title: Fast and satisfying dinners for your busiest evenings
item.description: From frittata to one-pot pasta, these speedy dinners are perfect for busy evenings and worth adding to your regular rotation.

item.title: For St George Illawarra, Shane Flanagan's dismissal is a new version of the same old story
item.description: St George Illawarra might have sacked coach Shane Flanagan amid a record losing streak, but such struggles have become sadly familiar to the joint venture.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos injury list grows, with Payne Haas to miss up to eight weeks, and Corey Jensen in hospital
item.description: Brisbane's injury crisis escalates, with Payne Haas to miss six to eight weeks and fellow prop Corey Jensen in hospital with a blood clot.

item.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child in 1960s
item.description: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

item.title: Fair Work Commission orders major retailers to help truck drivers with rising fuel costs
item.description: The Fair Work Commission orders that retailers, miners and other companies that use truck drivers and transport operators must hold twice-monthly reviews of fuel prices and adjust their rates accordingly.

item.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed
item.description: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

item.title: Gavin Preston's accused killer searched for 'no extradition countries' after shooting, prosecutors say
item.description: The Supreme Court has been told of the alleged internet searches made by a man accused of a brazen daylight cafe shooting in Melbourne's north-west.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Japan warns of slightly increased risk of mega-quake after 7.7-magnitude one
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.7 struck off the north-eastern coast of Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting about 170,000 people.

item.title: Woman saw childcare worker accused of rape touching tongues with three-year-old child, court hears
item.description: Childcare worker Joshua James Capps has pleaded not guilty to three counts of indecent treatment of a child in his care, and one count of rape.

item.title: Carlton confirms Elijah Hollands is in hospital after concerning scenes on Thursday night
item.description: Carlton says young star Elijah Hollands has been admitted to hospital after suffering a mental health episode in Thursday night's match.

item.title: Waves swell in Hokkaido, Japan after magnitude 7.5 earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Keir Starmer says he was 'wrong' to appoint Mandelson to DC ambassador role
item.description: The crisis around the embattled British prime minister continues to grow, with Sir Keir Starmer facing questions from MPs about what he knew of the vetting process of Peter Mandelson.

item.title: Trump warns US military 'ready to go' as US-Iran peace talks loom — as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump has warned if a deal with Iran is not reached soon, US military action could resume.

item.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science
item.description: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

item.title: How to keep your dog happy living in an apartment
item.description: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

item.title: Labor faces gas tax pressure as inquiry heats up before budget
item.description: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

item.title: Australian women have overtaken men in prescribing rates for ADHD medication
item.description: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

item.title: Why Australia's big gas producers haven't jacked up domestic prices unlike during Ukraine war
item.description: After a decade and a half of mostly failed threats from three prime ministers and constant surveillance by the competition regulator, the east coast gas export giants are suddenly putting Australians first.

item.title: From cost blowouts to fraud and benefits, here's everything you need to know about the NDIS
item.description: With more changes surrounding the NDIS set to be announced, here’s everything you need to know about the scheme.

item.title: NRL limits contact in training in bid to reduce head injuries
item.description: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

item.title: From TikTok to Tongan prison: Australian deportee who imported drugs and called himself a Comanchero boss
item.description: Eneasi Taumoefolau's 2024 arrest on drugs charges ignited fears the notoriously violent outlaw Comancheros gang had infiltrated "the Friendly Islands".

item.title: In charts: Australia's relationship with cash over the past two decades
item.description: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama's post-season entrance among leading storylines as 2026 NBA playoffs begin
item.description: The first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs is officially underway. Here are the key things to look out for in each series.

item.title: Administrators for Liberty Bell Bay smelter optimistic buyer will be found, as workers face job decision
item.description: With Australian and international bidders apparently eyeing off the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter, the workers and their families just want to know they'll have a job at the end of it all.

item.title: Volunteers who helped find Celine Cremer plan new missing person search in Tasmanian wilderness
item.description: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Anxiety, confusion and a 'yawning gulf' between Iran and US as ceasefire nears end
item.description: Fears are rising that the relative calm of recent days is about to collapse as the fate of the on-off truce talks seems impossible to guess.

item.title: Indonesian fisher says poverty behind illegal fishing in Australian waters
item.description: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

item.title: How a Vietnamese bakery outlived numerous South Asian shops in Sydney's Little India for 30 years
item.description: Keeping a Vietnamese bakery afloat in the heart of Sydney's Little India for 30 years took quick-thinking from its shop owner.

item.title: Community calls for water quality over quantity, in Murray-Darling Basin Plan review
item.description: The first-ever review into the Murray-Darling Basin Plan is underway, and the St George community wants a better deal.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down and hand reins to iPhone maker's hardware leader
item.description: Apple has announced the chief executive who took over from Steve Jobs is to step down and has named his successor.

item.title: 'Star Wars is happening': $7b spend on counter-drone technology — as it happened
item.description: The federal government is funnelling $7 billion into technology that can defeat other drones.

item.title: Dean Young named St George Illawarra Dragons coach after Shane Flanagan's exit
item.description: After about 12 hours without a head coach, the Dragons appoint assistant Dean Young to lead the winless side for "at least" the rest of the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Turtles 'awaiting their fate' buried under mud at NSW wetlands after inflows halted
item.description: Researchers in north-west NSW are wading through thick mud in an attempt to save hundreds of turtles after the NSW government decided to stop supplying water to the protected wetland.

item.title: Turtles buried in mud at Gwydir Wetlands
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US, Philippine and Australian troops begin largest military exercise in 'real-world conditions'
item.description: Troops from the US, Philippines, Australia and Japan unite for the largest military exercise in "real-world conditions" to ensure security in the Indo-Pacific region. But China has criticised the drill.

item.title: YouTubers face police probe after filming inside Dezi Freeman's Thologolong hideout
item.description: YouTubers are being investigated for trespass after filming inside the converted shipping container where fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman hid after shooting dead two police officers.

item.title: New Zealand urges Wellington residents in low-lying areas to evacuate due to floods
item.description: People living in low-lying areas of New Zealand's capital city, Wellington, are urged to evacuate as heavy rain drenches the city.

item.title: Formula 1 to make rule changes for Miami Grand Prix after concerns over new engine regulations
item.description: The next Formula 1 race in Miami will be a testing and proving ground for a number of new rules aimed at improving performance and safety.

item.title: One dead and six injured after shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids
item.description: A Canadian tourist has died and at least six other people have been injured after a man opened fire while atop of one of the historic Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico.

item.title: Homes damaged, powerlines down in Bunbury as possible tornado or microburst rips through city
item.description: Winds of more than 100 kilometres an hour swept along WA's southern coast last night, with serious damage reported in Bunbury and Esperance.

item.title: Who is John Ternus, the hardware engineer appointed as new CEO of Apple?
item.description: The new CEO of Apple will be tasked with catching up in the AI race and continuing the company's legacy as a dominating force in the technology world.

item.title: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon survives confidence vote
item.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he has the support of his party after calling and winning a vote of confidence in his leadership.

item.title: What I learnt about myself after quitting TV for a month
item.description: It was partly an experiment in self-control and habit-breaking, but also a response to the unhappy realisation that watching shows had become my crutch to zone out.

item.title: Hobart council to replace shared e-scooters with e-bikes amid safety and compliance concerns
item.description: The City of Hobart said e-bikes were a safer alternative to the e-scooters, which have continued to draw safety concerns from residents and visitors.

item.title: Vietnam War veteran Garry Chad joins exclusive list of Medal for Gallantry recipients
item.description: Sergeant Garry Chad is awarded the Medal for Gallantry for his actions in Vietnam's Long Khahn Province in 1971 that saved several lives.

item.title: Second death of Aboriginal inmate in Bandyup Women's Prison sparks call for review
item.description: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

item.title: Elijah Hollands's dad posts message about Carlton player's mental health struggle
item.description: Elijah Hollands's father, former AFL player Ben Hollands, posts on social media pledging to help lift his son up and urging others to check on their friends if they are struggling.

item.title: Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales on AI and trust
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Warwick Thornton and Deborah Mailman on their new film Wolfram
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Earthquake hits off coast of Timor, geophysics agency says
item.description: The epicentre was at a depth of 31 kilometres but there was no tsunami risk from the magnitude-6 quake, according to Indonesia's geophysics agency.

item.title: Israel's occupation of south Lebanon spells 'bleak' future despite ceasefire, experts say
item.description: Experts say Israel's continued presence in south Lebanon is creating a "bleak" future as it destroys villages in what it is calling a "buffer zone" and a "Forward Defense Line".

item.title: ACCC says Woolworths 'contravened' own guardrails for 'Prices Dropped' promotion, court hears
item.description: The consumer watchdog has taken Woolworths to court, claiming shoppers weren't receiving genuine discounts on hundreds of items.

item.title: The Drop music festival still owes ticketholders refunds, six years after cancelled shows
item.description: Ticketholders are still waiting for refunds and answers from The Drop music festival.

item.title: Man sentenced for pretending to shoot people while on Bondi Beach terror attack bridge
item.description: A man who yelled antisemitic profanities while pretending to shoot at people from a pedestrian bridge used by the Bondi Beach terror attack gunmen has been sentenced.

item.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump's cabinet after abuse of power allegations
item.description: The Labor secretary's departure was announced by a White House aide on Monday, local time.

item.title: Rebel Wilson requested website describing producer as an 'Indian Ghislaine Maxwell', court hears
item.description: Australian actor Rebel Wilson asked her crisis communications firm to publish a website that accused a producer on her own film of being an "Indian Ghislaine Maxwell", a Sydney court hears.

item.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after state election
item.description: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

item.title: China opens the gate to more Aussie beef with new export licences for several facilities
item.description: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

item.title: Government invests in counter-drone technology citing lessons of Iran war
item.description: The Australian government signs two contracts to develop counter-drone technology, including a laser that can track an object the size of a 10-cent coin travelling at more than 100 km/h.

item.title: El Salvador holds mass trial of nearly 500 alleged MS-13 gang members
item.description: The attorney-general says 486 suspected MS-13 members have faced trial for 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022.

item.title: States and territories left in the dark on NDIS savings
item.description: State treasurers say a meeting on NDIS reforms to be announced tomorrow was light on details, leaving states and territories waiting with everyone else to find out how a new round of savings measures will affect the disability scheme — and their budgets.

item.title: Wollongong researchers working to create shark-deterrent surfboard fins
item.description: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

item.title: Tom Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Elijah Hollands episode
item.description: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

item.title: Vanuatu denies it is about to sign a security deal with China as Australia works to ink its own pact
item.description: As Australia presses on to finally sign a key agreement with Vanuatu, reports of a security agreement with China have drawn a fierce response from Port Vila.

item.title: Two teenagers sentenced for roles in Ellenbrook school stabbing, planned as social media 'game'
item.description: Two boys are sentenced for their roles in the stabbing of a 15-year-old boy at a Perth high school, offending the prosecutor said was part of a "game" formulated online.

item.title: Former civil servant says there was political pressure to approve Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to US
item.description: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

item.title: Queensland Tourism Industry Council calls on government for support as tourists cancel bookings
item.description: A Far North Queensland caravan park manager says more people are ringing up to cancel than to book a spot with concerns over fuel prices and availability.

item.title: Premier Coal set to reduce production, cut jobs from Collie mine
item.description: Premier Coal confirms job losses at its Collie operation, with workers set to be informed of the details.

item.title: Captain stranded in the Strait of Hormuz tells of being trapped amid conflict
item.description: For months seafarers have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, tanker captain Raman Kapoor is speaking out about the fears and uncertainty of his crew.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Captain stranded near Strait of Hormuz speaks out
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: First responders unable to help mother bleeding in Darwin shelter for almost an hour, inquest hears
item.description: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

item.title: Man remains in critical condition with serious head injuries after 'violent incident' in Ngukurr
item.description: A man remains in a critical condition in hospital, and three others have been charged by police, after a "violent" altercation in the remote Northern Territory community of Ngukurr.

item.title: Government backs down on charging older Australians $50/hour for showers
item.description: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

item.title: Japan overhauls defence export rule to sell arms to 17 countries, including Australia
item.description: Japan has overhauled its decades-long defence export rule, allowing the country to sell lethal weapons internationally amid rising global tensions.

item.title: Live: Trump extends ceasefire with Iran, Vance cancels Pakistan trip
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he is extending the ceasefire with Iran. Follow live.

item.title: Whether it's grief or liberation, family estrangement affects many of us
item.description: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians have been affected by it.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 22: We're off for a trip around the world
item.description: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

item.title: Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art sees how 24 artists working across the country Yield Strength
item.description: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

item.title: Butler urged to end 'limbo' for 500,000 Australians with psychosocial disability in NDIS reforms
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

item.title: Support services nationwide 'buckling under demand' during Sexual Assault Awareness Month
item.description: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

item.title: Victorians transitioning from gas exacerbates growing problem of undervoltage
item.description: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

item.title: Closure of AEIOU centres for children with autism after NDIS cuts leaves lives upended
item.description: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

item.title: Carpooling helps to slash fuel prices, so why aren't more people doing it?
item.description: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

item.title: Junior clubs and neuroscientists welcome new NRL guidelines to limit head knocks but say it's not enough
item.description: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

item.title: Fourth man charged over alleged kidnapping murder of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian
item.description: The 85-year-old's abduction triggered an 11-day search, with his remains later found near a golf course in Pitt Town, in the city's north-west.

item.title: Refurbished facilities and funding for new units locked in for Hobart Women's Shelter
item.description: A women's shelter struggling to meet enormous demand says it will soon be able to almost double its capacity.

item.title: FOI documents reveal Adelaide's COP31 considerations included gift bags and 'flotels'
item.description: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

item.title: A rare glimpse inside the services helping NSW women leaving custody get clean
item.description: Guthrie House is one of the few residential rehabilitation services in NSW designed to help women with current involvement with the criminal justice system get back on track.

item.title: Donald Trump extends Iran ceasefire but continues Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will hold off attacking Iran until Tehran's negotiating teams come up with a unified proposal for a peace deal.

item.title: What 100 years of Australian soil samples in our national archive can tell us about our history and future
item.description: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

item.title: Australian-first lawsuit over 'extreme heat' risks in remote NT public housing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Papunya residents living in extreme heat sue NT government over unsafe housing
item.description: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

item.title: Newcastle homebuyers push for reform after off-the-plan development leaves lives in 'limbo'
item.description: Five years after signing contracts and paying their deposits, families say their lives have been put on hold as they wait to move into their "forever homes" in Newcastle.
